name: 350.org
mission: 350.org is building a global grassroots climate movement that can hold our leaders accountable to the realities of science and the principles of justice. That movement is rising from the bottom up all over the world, and is uniting to create the solutions that will ensure a better future for all. Our online campaigns, grassroots organizing, and mass public actions bring together a global network active in over 188 countries
theoryOfChange: 350.org believes that by building a global grassroots movement (people power), they can dismantle the influence of the fossil fuel industry (resistance) and accelerate the transition to community-led renewable energy (solutions).

keyConcerns.headline: 95% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

keyConcerns.headline: CEO compensation is 2.2% of revenue ($434,889 on $19,507,313)
keyConcerns.detail: For orgs with revenue $5-50M, CEO pay above 2% of revenue warrants scrutiny.

keyConcerns.headline: Fundraising costs are 32% of total expenses
keyConcerns.detail: High fundraising spend reduces funds available for programs.

judge_issues.message: The risk summary only mentions the high domestic burn rate, but the risk section also includes concerns about CEO compensation and fundraising costs. The summary should mention all key risks.
The impact rationale mentions a high data confidence level, but this is not directly related to the impact score itself. It's more relevant to the data confidence section.

all_citations.claim: The organization maintains audited financials and significant assets to support its operations.
The organization accepts general charitable donations and sadaqah-eligible giving for climate action.
350.org reported total revenue of $19,507,313 and focuses on US campaigning and training programs.
The charity holds a 99.66666666666667/100 score, with a program expense ratio of 81.5%, 7.2 months of working capital, and fundraising efficiency of $0.12 per $1 raised.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: 350.org demonstrates excellent financial health and transparency, though its focus on advocacy rather than direct service results in a moderate alignment score for specific donor profiles.
areas_for_improvement: Increasing direct service impact metrics alongside advocacy efforts
Enhancing specific alignment for donors seeking traditional faith-based frameworks

dimension_explanations.alignment: With an alignment score of 22/50, the organization offers a medium fit for donors, focusing on the urgent cause of climate advocacy [5]. While it is a sadaqah-eligible option for those supporting environmental justice, its mission is centered on systemic policy change rather than traditional religious service delivery [3][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization achieves a high impact score of 39/50 by directing 81.5% of its $19,507,313 revenue toward core programs like training and mobilization [4][5]. Its financial stability is evidenced by 7.2 months of working capital and a high data confidence level [2][5].

baseline_narrative.headline: 350.org is a high-impact climate advocacy organization dedicated to mobilizing global movements to challenge systemic environmental threats.
strengths: Exceptional financial efficiency with a program expense ratio of 81.5%
Strong liquidity with 7.2 months of working capital available
Highly efficient fundraising operations spending only $0.12 per $1 raised
baseline_narrative.summary: 350.org works to inspire and train a diverse climate movement through programs focused on US campaigning and global climate solutions [4][5]. The organization maintains a strong financial position with a total revenue of $19,507,313 and a high Charity Navigator score of 99.66666666666667/100 [4][5].

rich_narrative.headline: 350.org is a high-impact climate advocacy organization dedicated to mobilizing global movements to challenge systemic environmental threats.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize systemic climate advocacy and value highly transparent, financially efficient organizations with a proven track record in movement building.
donor_motivations: Want to challenge the systems leading to catastrophic climate change
Value high financial efficiency and low fundraising overhead
Prefer supporting established advocacy groups with global reach
Interested in fossil fuel divestment and climate finance reform
giving_considerations: Donations are for sadaqah and do not fulfill zakat obligations
Impact is measured through policy shifts and movement growth rather than direct aid
Recent revenue trends show a moderate decline in annual funding
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those who prefer direct humanitarian relief like food or medical aid.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects a verified theory of change and high transparency, though specific cost-per-beneficiary data is missing.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: 350.org inspires and trains a global movement to challenge fossil fuel systems and promote renewable energy solutions through grassroots organizing.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that advocacy efforts are effectively mobilizing communities and influencing climate policy.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: A negative revenue CAGR suggests a significant need for new funding to maintain global advocacy scale.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Global, with focus on US and 10+ other countries

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 81.5%</cite>, which significantly exceeds the <cite id="10">industry standard of 75%</cite>.
350.org holds a <cite id="8">99.66666666666667/100 rating from Charity Navigator</cite>, including <cite id="11">perfect scores of 100 for Culture and Leadership</cite>.
The charity demonstrates high financial resilience with <cite id="12">7.2 months of working capital</cite> available to sustain operations.

strengths_deep_dive: 350.org's operational model is built on lean fundraising and high program output. Its <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.12 per $1 raised</cite> is among the best in the advocacy sector. Furthermore, the organization maintains <cite id="13">100% independent board members</cite>, ensuring objective oversight of its <cite id="3">$19.5 million in annual revenue</cite>. This governance structure supports a <cite id="14">documented theory of change</cite> that has been verified by external evaluators, providing donors with confidence in the strategic direction of their contributions.
rich_narrative.summary: 350.org is a high-impact climate advocacy organization dedicated to mobilizing global movements to challenge systemic environmental threats. Founded in <cite id="1">2008</cite>, the organization has grown into a global force for climate justice, focusing on <cite id="2">grassroots organizing, fossil fuel divestment, and climate finance advocacy</cite>. The organization maintains a strong financial position, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $19,587,115</cite> in its most recent filing. 350.org is distinguished by its <cite id="4">exceptional financial efficiency</cite>, directing <cite id="5">81.5% of its expenses toward program services</cite>. This high efficiency is bolstered by a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.12 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor contributions directly support its mission of inspiring and training a diverse climate movement. The organization's commitment to transparency is evidenced by its <cite id="7">Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and a near-perfect <cite id="8">overall score of 99.99.66666666666667/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>. While its operations are global, its financial footprint includes significant <cite id="9">grantmaking activity totaling $1,860,387</cite>, supporting both domestic and foreign partners in the fight against catastrophic climate change.
